name: Tomato
description: Ripe red tomato flesh (Solanum lycopersicum) is not significantly toxic to cats in small amounts, but the green parts of the tomato plant — leaves, stems, sprouts, and unripe green tomatoes — contain solanine and tomatine (glycoalkaloids). These decrease as the fruit ripens to red, which is why ripe tomato flesh carries minimal risk.
safetyNotes: Avoid: all green plant parts — leaves, stems, vines, and unripe tomatoes contain solanine and tomatine
Ripe red flesh: safe in very small amounts; no nutritional benefit for cats and high acid content may cause mild upset
Symptoms if green parts ingested: drooling, vomiting, diarrhea, lethargy, weakness; severe cases cause bradycardia or CNS depression
Never feed tomato sauce, ketchup, or canned tomato products — typically contain garlic, onion, and salt
Contact your vet if your cat eats significant amounts of green tomato plant material

symptoms.name: Hypersalivation (Drooling)
symptoms.onset: Within a few hours of ingestion.
symptoms.notes: This is typically one of the first signs of oral and gastrointestinal irritation caused by the plant's glycoalkaloid toxins. 2. Vomiting and Diarrhea

symptoms.name: Vomiting and diarrhea
symptoms.onset: Within a few hours of ingestion.
symptoms.notes: Solanine and tomatine act as direct gastrointestinal irritants. If the vomiting or diarrhea is severe, it can lead to dehydration. 3. Lethargy and Weakness

symptoms.name: Lethargy, weakness, and confusion
symptoms.onset: Progresses after gastrointestinal signs (usually within a few to several hours).
symptoms.notes: This is caused by the systemic absorption of solanine, which acts as a cholinesterase inhibitor and depresses the central nervous system. 4. Dilated Pupils (Mydriasis)

symptoms.name: Dilated pupils (mydriasis)
symptoms.notes: This is an additional neurological effect stemming from the disruption of the nervous system by the glycoalkaloid toxins. 5. Slowed Heart Rate (Bradycardia)

symptoms.name: Slowed heart rate (bradycardia)
symptoms.onset: Occurs after the systemic absorption of a large toxic dose.
symptoms.notes: Severe cardiovascular and neurological effects are rare in cats. Felines typically do not consume enough of the unpalatable green plant material to trigger life-threatening symptoms, but it remains a risk in cases of severe poisoning.
